bones.common_name: Bones (Edible Raw Meaty Bones for GSD Diet)
bones.validation_result: 5 bone ingredients added, systemic gaps remain (biotin, chloride)
bones.notes: Edible raw meaty bones suitable for GSD DIY raw diet. Ca:P ratios range 1.2:1 to 2:1. USDA FDC data is meat-only; calcium from Monica Segal whole-RMB data. Amino acids/vitamins scaled by meat%.

ingredients.display_name: Pescoço de Turco Cru (Whole Turkey Neck, Raw, Bone-In)

protein_g.note: Whole RMB (bone-in) from DogsFirst.ie USDA-verified data

calcium_mg.note: Whole RMB Ca from Monica Segal (bone=45% of RMB)

phosphorus_mg.note: Whole RMB P from DogsFirst.ie range midpoint

potassium_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 55% meat + bone trace contribution

sodium_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 55% meat + bone trace contribution

magnesium_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 55% meat + bone trace contribution

iron_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 55% meat + bone trace contribution

zinc_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 55% meat + bone trace contribution

copper_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 55% meat + bone trace contribution

manganese_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 55% meat + bone trace contribution

selenium_ug.note: USDA meat-only × 55% meat + bone trace contribution

vitamin_k_ug.reason: Vitamin K negligible in raw bone tissue; primary dietary source is plant-based

vitamin_c_mg.reason: Vitamin C negligible in raw meat/bone tissue; dogs synthesize endogenously

biotin_ug.note: Biotin negligible in raw bone tissue; estimated zero until Frida/CoFID reference available

iodine_ug.note: Iodine near zero in raw meaty bone; geographic variability makes precise measurement unreliable

chloride_mg.note: Chloride not measured for bone-in RMB; estimated zero pending CoFID reference

safety_alerts.message: Turkey neck is fully edible for GSDs. Cut into segments for dogs that gulp.
Ca:P ratio ~1.98:1 — exceeds NRC 1.4:1 max for growth. Combine with low-Ca ingredients.

metadata.usda_description: Turkey, whole, neck, meat only, raw (USDA base; Ca/P adjusted for whole bone-in RMB)

ingredients.display_name: Pescoço de Frango Cru (Chicken Neck, Raw, with Skin, Bone-In)

protein_g.note: Whole RMB (bone-in) from DogsFirst.ie USDA-verified data

calcium_mg.note: Whole RMB Ca from Monica Segal (bone=36% of RMB)

phosphorus_mg.note: Whole RMB P from DogsFirst.ie range midpoint

sodium_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 64% meat + bone trace contribution

magnesium_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 64% meat + bone trace contribution

iron_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 64% meat + bone trace contribution

zinc_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 64% meat + bone trace contribution

copper_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 64% meat + bone trace contribution

manganese_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 64% meat + bone trace contribution

vitamin_k_ug.reason: Vitamin K negligible in raw bone tissue; primary dietary source is plant-based

safety_alerts.message: Chicken neck may be gulped whole by large dogs. Cut into 1-2 inch segments before feeding.

metadata.usda_description: Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat and skin and giblets and neck, raw (USDA base; Ca/P adjusted for whole bone-in neck)

protein_g.note: Whole RMB (bone-in) from DogsFirst.ie USDA-verified data

calcium_mg.note: Whole RMB Ca from Monica Segal (bone=30% of RMB)

phosphorus_mg.note: Whole RMB P from DogsFirst.ie range midpoint

sodium_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 70% meat + bone trace contribution

magnesium_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 70% meat + bone trace contribution

zinc_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 70% meat + bone trace contribution

copper_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 70% meat + bone trace contribution

manganese_mg.note: USDA meat-only × 70% meat + bone trace contribution

selenium_ug.note: USDA meat-only × 70% meat + bone trace contribution

vitamin_a_iu.reason: Vitamin A negligible in pork muscle tissue; liver is the primary dietary source

vitamin_k_ug.reason: Vitamin K negligible in raw bone tissue; primary dietary source is plant-based

vitamin_c_mg.reason: Vitamin C negligible in raw meat/bone tissue; dogs synthesize endogenously

biotin_ug.note: Biotin negligible in raw bone tissue; estimated zero until Frida/CoFID reference available

iodine_ug.note: Iodine near zero in raw meaty bone; geographic variability makes precise measurement unreliable

chloride_mg.note: Chloride not measured for bone-in RMB; estimated zero pending CoFID reference

safety_alerts.message: Pork rib bones are softer than beef but not all dogs will consume entire bone. Verify full consumption.
Ca:P ratio ~1.99:1 — exceeds NRC max for growth. Use sparingly, balance with low-Ca ingredients.

metadata.usda_description: Pork, fresh, loin, country-style ribs, bone-in, separable lean and fat, raw (USDA SR Legacy; Ca/P adjusted for whole bone-in rib)
